description:

description:
Please complete the following for our class today:

1) Read the Freemark Abbey Winery case from the course packet

2) Complete Case #1 Writeup

3) Review information for the final group project document

4) Make a plan with your team to approach and choose a final project company.
name: 1/20 Deliverables

description:
Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work. Remember, you are not allowed to use generative AI in any way on case writeup assignments. 

These case assignments are developed to help you foster your strategic thinking skills, and the process is more important than the outcome (i.e., did you get the "right" answers). Embrace the process and have a long view as you work through this and future cases! 

1. Assuming Mr. Jaeger chooses to harvest the Riesling grapes before the storm arrives, how much money will he make? Quantify this as clearly as possible. 

2. Assuming Mr. Jaeger chooses to leave the grapes on the vine, what is the probability that the grapes will end up with botrytis, and how much money will he make if that occurs?  

3. Imagine you a consultant for Mr Jaeger, brought on to advise him in light of the impending storm. What would you advise Mr Jaeger to do? Justify your answer as strongly as possible.
name: Case Writeup #1: Freemark Abbey Winery

rubric.long_description: How much money will Mr Jaeger make if he harvests before the storm arrives? This question has a clear quantitative answer.

ratings.long_description: Correctly identified 12,000 bottles at $2.85 selling price. This results in $34,200. Some flexibility provided if students state assumptions that change these calculations, if these assumptions are reasonable.

rubric.long_description: Assuming Mr. Jaeger chooses to leaves the grapes on the vine, what is the probability of botrytis, and how much money will he make if that occurs?

ratings.long_description: If Mr Jaeger leaves the grapes on the vine, then there is a 50% chance of a storm. If the storm hits, then there is a 40% change of botrytis forming. So the probability is (0.5)*(0.4)=0.2 or 20%. The case states these will sell for $8 a bottle. But it results in 30% less juice. So the calculation is $8 * 12,000*(0.70) = 67,200. Some flexibility for alternative answers and approaches if reasonable assumptions are stated.

rubric.long_description: What would you advise Mr. Jaeger to do and why?

ratings.long_description:
Full marks given for any answer that has a reasonable logic based on clear analysis of the case data/facts. Students could note that if he doesn't harvest, the expected value is higher ($39,744 vs $34,200), so he should wait it out. Other answers may reason that he should harvest because he is seeking to minimize risk, reputational loss, etc. Any well-reasoned and justified recommendation based on sound analysis is acceptable here. 

1 point for opinion only with no justification rooted in case facts. 2 points for a superficial answer with only a weak justification based on limited case facts. 3 points for answers where students have engaged with the case facts strongly but develop only a superficial recommendation. 4 points for a strong recommendation with a compelling justification.

description:
Please complete the following for our class today:

1) Read the course pack reading on Setting aspirations, mission, vision, and values. This is a long reading, so focus on reading it quickly while pulling out the most important points. 

2) Read through the Atomic Financial Case HERE Download HERE. This is a short case, and focus on whether you think Jordan should cancel the contract or not, and why.  

3) Complete Case #2 Writeup

4) Review information for the final group project document

5) Complete the first Strategy and YOUR Life Journal entry. Note that you do NOT need to submit this--it is a journaling assignment and is related to this session.
name: 1/22 Deliverables

description:
After you complete the reading on Setting Aspirations from the course pack, answer the following questions for this case writeup: 

1) What have you found in life that makes you truly happy and helps you find joy? 

2) What is your plan to achieve happiness now and in the future? 

3) Craft mission, vision, and values statements for you individually.

4)  Would you advise Jordan to cancel the contract? Explain why or why not.
name: Case Writeup #2: Setting Aspirations

ratings.long_description: +2 points for evidence of full engagement with the question. Points subtracted for partial or missing answers.
+2 points for evidence of full engagement with the question. Points subtracted for underdeveloped, partial, or missing answers. Note that this "plan" to achieve happiness is a strategy.
+3 points for evidence of full engagement with the question. Should have developed mission, vision, and values statements. Points subtracted for underdeveloped, partial, or missing answers.

For full credit students should take a strong stand on whether Jordan should cancel the contract or not, and explain using case facts and analysis. Strong answers should realize the tradeoff between the financial and social goals of the organization, which are aligned long-term but in conflict in the short-term. They should also notice stakeholder pressures on Jordan from multiple different stakeholders. Strong answers should acknowledge the work within the complexity of the situation to articulate why their chosen direction makes the most sense. +2 points for underdeveloped answers that don't engage with case facts and analysis in a strong way. +1 point for superficial answers based on opinion alone.

description:
Apply what you have learned so far about organizational aspirations to your personal life by recording responses to these or other related questions in your personal strategy journal.

  1. What are your 3-5 overall life goals? Start crafting a mission, vision, and values for you personally.

  2. How do mission, vision, and values affect the kind of company you hope to work for (or start) in the future?

  3. How might mission, vision, and values enhance the organizations you currently participate in (family, non-profits, clubs, etc.)?

  4. What are some scriptures or conference talks that inspire you and speak to these questions above?
name: Strategy and your life journal entry #1

description:
1) Review the Module 1 Summary HERE (to be posted after the end of the first module)

2) Read the case Dogfight Over Europe: RyanAir (A) from the class packet

3) Complete Case Question Writeup #3

4) Look through this helpful resource to improve your ability to construct and use logic/issue trees: https://www.craftingcases.com/issue-tree-guide/
name: 1/27 Deliverables

description:
Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work.

1. Assume you are a consultant brought in to advise the Ryan brothers on their entry strategy. Do you think it is likely to succeed? Why or why not?

2. Can the Ryan brothers make profits at the 98 Irish pound fare they propose? Support your answer using quantitative cost analysis analysis while specifying carefully your assumptions. 

3. How do you expect Aer Lingus and British Airways to respond to RyanAir's entry? Why?**

**For this final question, utilize the Competitive Response Profile framework found HERE Download HERE. This is a framework used to systematically think through 1) what drives a competitor, 2) what is the competitor doing (or able to do), and 3) the likely response of a competitor to the moves of another competitor (in this case a potential competitor). This tool can be used when there are a few strong competitors and likely competitive response is key to anticipate. Create a Competitive Response Profile for both Aer Lingus and British Airways to help answer this question.
name: Case Writeup #3: RyanAir

rubric.long_description: For full credit a strong justification should be provided for why the entry strategy is likely to succeed or not. This should be based on quantitative or qualitative analysis and engagement with case facts, and not on opinion alone. The answer could argue either way. 3 points for a strong justification as outlined above. 2 points if the justification is partial or based on limited analysis. 1 point for a response based on opinion alone. 0 points for no response.

3 points possible for conducting quantitative analysis on RyanAir's cost structure and ability to turn a profit at 98 Irish Pounds. This answer should clearly articulate assumptions (e.g., 100% load factor and if it is possible, matching American airlines' efficiency, etc.) which are key to understanding where the cost differences stem from. Students might also reference demand considerations, about whether or not train, ferry, and existing RyanAir customers may use RyanAir. Points taken off if assumptions not clearly articulated, for partial analysis, and for an answer that does not clearly answer the question.

4 points possible for this question. 1 point for well-reasoned response explaining what British Airways is likely to do. 1 point for well-reasoned response explaining what Aer Lingus is likely to do. 2 points for evidence (with a figure or written out in analysis) that the competitive response profile framework was used to formulate the responses (1 point for each).

description:
Prepare the following for this class:

1) Read the Case ColaWars Continue: Coke and Pepsi in 2010.

2) Complete Case Question Writeup #4

The Excel tool for Five Forces Analysis is found HERE
name: 2/3 Deliverables

description:
The Five Forces Framework is used to assess the structural attractiveness of an industry from the perspective of an incumbent. For this case writeup submission, use the "five forces tool" found under course content or HERE to conduct a 5 forces analysis of the consumer soft drink (CSD producer) industry. Note that this does NOT include the bottlers--just for Coke, Pepsi, and their competitors (the CSD industry). Fill in the initial boxes and each of the lines/ratings (wherever possible). Feel free to adjust the weighting on certain dimensions if you think something is more/less important.

After completing this framework, what is your overall assessment about the attractiveness of this industry? Justify your answer. What forces (if any) are particularly concerning and what are the major drivers? Which are not concerning? 

 

Now, use the "five forces tool" to conduct a 5 forces analysis of the bottling industry (try to do this one more quickly--we will cover it in less depth in class). After completing this framework, what is your overall assessment about the attractiveness of this industry? Why do you think bottling has historically been a less attractive industry than CSD producing?  

 

The PESTLE analysis is used to think about potential futures that might influence an industry's profitability or a company's ability to compete in that industry. Using this framework, and the information presented in the case, what factor(s) do you see that are particularly concerning for CSD producers looking forward?
name: Case Writeup #4: Cola Wars

description:
We have been studying external analysis and theory (5 forces, PESTLE, value chain, etc.) For this strategy journal assignment, I invite you to think and write about the following questions to apply strategy to YOUR life:

  1. Are you focusing your efforts in the "right" arenas? How do you know where the "right" places are? Where should you be focusing more or less effort? 
  2. How does this idea of "competing in a competitive arena" differ in the Gospel of Jesus Christ? What does success look like? 
  3. How can you add more unique value in the industries or environments in which you are "competing" (i.e., spending your time)? 
  4. In reading the scriptures and listening to the living prophets, what futures do you see that you should prepare for? What are some things you should start (or stop) this year to prepare for these futures? 
  5. Any additional scriptures, conference talks, etc. that speak to this topic?
name: Strategy and your life journal entry #2

description:
Today we do not have in-person class. Instead, use the time to meet with your team to work on the final project. I suggest you use your time to: 

1) Complete and submit the team contract assignment (one per group). 

2) Continue to work on selecting a company to work with on the final project. The first presentation is in less than a month, so you need to quickly make progress on selecting and gathering data for the strategic audit on your company. 

3) Begin working on your statement of work/engagement letter. Examples are shown on Canvas. Outline the work streams and scope the project as a team and with the company. Identify what how you will collect data, what you need from the company, relevant deliverables, and important dates. 

4) Begin working on the strategic audit. You need to 1) define the strategy to understand and clearly articulate what the company is trying to achieve and how it positions itself in the industry, 2) analyze the external environment (industry analysis) to identify opportunities and threats in the company’s external environment, 3) perform an internal analysis to understand what the company does well and where it falls short in leveraging its resources and capabilities, and to understand how it positions itself to provide unique value, and 4) assess strategic alignment and evaluate key performance metrics. This audit should lead to a clear synthesis and articulation of evidence-backed strategic problems that the company must address. You want to distill these down to what you think is the "crux" that needs to be addressed. 

See you all on Thursday for the RoboTech case!
name: 2/10 Deliverables

description:
A team contract is an agreement between you and your teammates about how your team will operate during this course. It outlines the agreed upon rules and standards that will govern your interactions. It sets ground rules for participation, communication, conflict resolution, decision-making, meeting frequency and expectations, client interaction, etc. It also outlines what "success" looks like for the team, and how team contributions should be assessed at the end of the semester. 

Please draft and submit a team contract. Below are some areas and questions to consider. Your contract does not have to include all the areas below (this is YOUR team after all), but remember that a skimpy contract will have limited power when problems arise. 

Goals

  • What are the goals of the team?
  • What are your personal goals for this assignment?
  • What kind of obstacles might you encounter in reaching your goals?
  • What happens if all of you decide you want to get an A grade, but because of time constraints, one person decides that a B will be acceptable?
  • Is it acceptable for one or two team members to do more work than the others in order to get the team an A?
  • Will the team follow a more independent process (division of labor) or work in a more collaborative mode? 
  • What should the timing be of key deliverables? 

Meeting Norms

  • Do you have a preference for when meetings will be held? Do you have a preference for where/how they should be held? 
  • What if a member cannot attend? 
  • How often do you think the team will need to meet outside of class? How long do you anticipate meetings will be?
  • How will you record and distribute the minutes and action lists produced by each meeting?

Work Norms

  • How much time per week do you anticipate it will take to make the project successful?
  • How will work be divided among team members?
  • How will deadlines be set?
  • How will you decide who should do which tasks?
  • Where will you record who is responsible for which tasks?
  • What will happen if someone does not follow through on a commitment (e.g., missing a deadline, not showing up to meetings)?
  • How will the work be reviewed?
  • What happens if people have different opinions on the quality of the work?
  • What will you do if one or more team members are not doing their share of the work?
  • How will you deal with different work habits of individual team members (e.g., some people like to get assignments done as early as possible; others like to work under the pressure of a deadline)?

Decision Making

  • Do you need consensus (100% approval of all team members) before making a decision?
  • What will you do if one of you fixates on a particular idea?
  • How will you handle disagreements?
  • Who will be in charge of which aspects of the team? 

Client Interaction

  • What rules will dictate interactions with your client? 
  • Who will be the main point of contact for the team with the client? 

Communication

  • How can you ensure positive and efficient communication between meetings? 
  • How can you ensure positive and efficient communication in meetings? 
  • How often do you expect to communicate? 
  • What types of communication are out of bounds?
  • What norms and conventions will you adopt to make sure everyone is heard?  

Evaluation

  • To get a perfect peer contribution score from each team member, what is required? 
  • What feedback mechanisms will you adopt to help team members know how they are doing? 
  • What behaviors clearly reduce the peer contribution score?
name: Final Group Project Team Contract

description:
Prior to the start of class, please upload an answer via Canvas to the following questions. You may work in groups, although the final submission should be your own work.

1) With the information available to Chen in 2009, do you think her decision to invest $45 million to diversify RoboTech into medical robotics was appropriate? Why or why not?

2) Do a resource/capability audit for Robotech, as well as an activity audit (firm value chain). What resources, capabilities, and activities are important for them to succeed?   

3) How would you evaluate the company's first three years in the US medical device market? What do you think it has done most effectively? Where has it fallen short? 

4) What should Chen do now? How should the company move forward into 2017 and beyond? Which, if any of the proposed capital investments should it make?
name: Case Writeup #5: RoboTech--Storming the US Market

description:
Prepare the following for our class today:

1) Read the case Aldi: The Dark Horse Discounter in the course packet

2) Complete Case Question Writeup #6
name: 2/19 Deliverables

description:
This case writeup has you collect and analyze outside data to better understand Aldi's strategy and the strength of its competitive positioning. If you don't do the case writeup, then just look through to get a strong sense of the pricing and reviews. 

1) Create a shopping basket for 5-7 common grocery items. Then compare pricing to buy this basket at both Aldi and Walmart. Report your findings in a table. Note that you can find and use “weekly ads” from Walmart and Aldi posted on their websites. You can also visit Walmart or Walmart.com to compare prices. Finding items first in Aldi’s ad, and then looking them up on Walmart.com (after selecting your store) may be quickest. My favorite Aldi store is in zip code 92508. Of course, make sure to use a consistent zip code in your pricing analysis.

Now check out 10 or so online reviews (use Google) for an Aldi store in the United States. What are consistent themes you find in the Aldi experience at that store? What do customers like about Aldi and what tradeoffs do they note in shopping at Aldi vs other grocers?

What does your analysis say about Aldi’s value stick relative to Walmart?

2) Put together a comparative cost analysis for Walmart vs Aldi. Does Aldi have a competitive advantage or disadvantage relative to Walmart? 

3) Articulate Aldi's strategy.

4) Given your above analyses, should Walmart be worried about Aldi? Should Aldi be worried about Walmart? 

5) Do you think Aldi should locate its stores close to Walmart stores, or further away? Why?
name: Case Writeup #6: Aldi--the Dark Horse Discounter

description:
Prepare the following for our class today:

1) Read the Ducati case from the course packet

2) Complete Case Question Writeup #7

3) Complete Strategy Journal Entry #3
name: 2/24 Deliverables

description:
Please respond to the following case preparation questions for our discussion on Ducati:

1) How did Ducati become the second most profitable motorcycle maker in the world despite its small scale? What is the fundamental logic of Minoli's turnaround? 

2) After you watch the video on constructing a strategy canvas (on learnstrategy.byu.edu), create a strategy canvas that shows value curves for Ducati pre-turnaround, Ducati post-turnaround, Harley, and Honda (you can lump other Japanese manufacturers with Honda). What does this highlight about the strategic shift by Minoli?

3) What are the key tangible differentiators and the key intangible differentiators for Ducati? How are these built over time?  

4) Can Ducati sustain its position in the sport segment? Can Honda and the other Japanese manufacturers stop its growth in this segment?

5) Imagine you are a consultant hired by Ducati in 2001 to help it grow and achieve its goals. What strategic alternative do you recommend Minoli should pursue in 2001, and why?
name: Case Writeup #7: Ducati

description:
The Lord told Joseph Smith, “For all have not every gift given unto them; for there are many gifts, and to every man is given a gift by the Spirit of God.

“To some is given one, and to some is given another, that all may be profited thereby.” (D&C 46:11–12 Links to an external site..)

In a parable the Savior told of a man who was preparing to go to a far country. Before leaving he entrusted to one servant five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent. The first two servants invested their talents and doubled them. The last servant fearfully buried his one talent in the earth. Upon returning, the traveler said to each of the first two servants, “Well done, thou good and faithful servant,” but to the servant who had buried his talent he said:

“Thou wicked and slothful servant, thou knewest that I reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:

“Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own money with usury.

“Take therefore the talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.

“And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.” (Matt. 25:26–28, 30 Links to an external site..)

What unique resources and capabilities do you possess, that can provide unique value in your sphere of influence? Are they VRIO? What could you do more to organize around them to exploit in the future (the "O" in VRIO)?
name: Strategy and your life journal entry #3

description:
Hi team. Class on 2/27 will be VIRTUAL. You can watch the class materials yourself or get together in your team to work through the exercises. 

I have posted two videos. One is on cost/economies of scale and other is on demand/elasticities/pricing. Each video corresponds to a spreadsheet assignment. This should be obvious in the materials. 

I have also posted a "differentiation advantage exercise" assignment with data on grocery stores. You can use these data if you want to practice calculating an NPS, constructing a strategy canvas, segmenting the market, and making a strategy group map. There is NOT a video on this, but this aligns with what you have seen in the other course videos. 

Please do the following: 

1) Watch the cost video and work through the Netflix assignment to construct a scale curve and understand how to use scale curves. Note—I recorded this video for another class, so ignore the first part where I talk about the exam, etc. Just focus on the content  

2) Watch the demand video and work through the demand assignment (Redbox data) to estimate a demand curve and do basic pricing analysis. 

3) Look through the differentiation advantage data and construct a strategy canvas and work through NPS.

All materials can be found HERELinks to an external site. and also under "Data Exercises" under "Course Content": 

You can work at your own pace and go as deep as you want. You will notice that I have posted the solutions, so the focus is on learning and not on performance. 

Finally, complete the QUIZ for the session. 

See you all next Tuesday in class!

description:
Please complete the following questions before our class discussion:

1) How attractive was the taxi industry to compete in (before Uber and other ride-hailing players entered the market)? Explain what you see as the key factors affecting market attractiveness.

2) What pain points were consumers AND drivers experiencing with the traditional taxi service? (You can use the Buyer Utility Map here).

3) How did a platform allow Uber to uniquely address these pain points compared to a traditional taxi company (pipeline model)?

4) Is the taxi industry (with these new platforms) likely a winner-take all industry? What would need to be true for Uber to gain most of the market share? 

5) Despite being highly disruptive, Uber has struggled to make money. Are you bullish or bearish on Uber’s long-term prospects? Explain why.
name: Case Writeup #8: Uber: Changing the Way the World Moves

description:
Please complete the following for class today:

1) Read the Netflix case from the course packet

2) Complete Case Question Writeup #9
name: 3/17 Deliverables

description:
Case preparation questions: 

1) What was Blockbuster's strategy before Netflix entered? Explain this as clearly as possible, as well as the key resources, capabilities, and activities.

2) Do you think Blockbuster had a sustainable competitive advantage? Justify your answer.

3) How did Netflix enter the market and gain a foothold? Was Blockbuster "dumb" to allow them to have this entry point?

4) **see note below** What was innovative about Netflix's strategy and business model? Why was it so problematic for Blockbuster?

5) Compare Blockbuster's and Netflix's profit models. What insights does this provide into the company's strategies? 

 

To help with Question 4: Innovative strategies can fundamentally break the current trade-off between differentiation and low cost, and lead to value innovations.

Review the "four actions framework" and the "ERRC grid" at this website: https://www.blueoceanstrategy.com/tools/four-actions-framework/Links to an external site.

Then, using this framework, analyze Netflix's strategy and choices to determine how it initially created a value innovation. Submit your analysis for the case writeup via Canvas.
name: Case Writeup #9: Netflix

description:
There is NO IN PERSON class today. Instead, spend time with your team to push forward your final project. 

Also, sign up as a team (at the following link) to meet with me in my office to receive coaching on your final project.

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1LQSFCXykQegfVeiQz4AaipYDyIuEeb4ei-jAHUpZoUg/edit?usp=sharing
name: 3/19 Deliverables

description: To free up time to study for the final exam, you do not have a case writeup for Tuesday's session. Instead, the expectation is that you carefully read and think about the case before our discussion in class. After you have read the case, indicate in this "quiz" you have prepared by answering the single T/F question.
name: Quiz #6: Shein vs Zara: Digital Transformation in the Fast-Fashion Industry

description:
Throughout the semester we have seen that strategy is about tradeoffs. It is about choosing what to do, but also about choosing what NOT to do. Leaders in organizations consequently have to have careful "no" criterion set so that they focus on what is most important. In corporate strategy we have seen that it is easy to say YES, to a merger and acquisition based on only limited synergies. Or YES to vertically integrating when there is little rationale for doing so. In business strategy we saw that is is easy to say YES to trying to serve all customer needs or customers, or to trying to delivering value in all ways. But strategy is fundamentally about saying NO to the right things.

Ponder on what your "no" criterion are in your life. That is, if something doesn't satisfy X, Y, and/or Z I choose to say NO so that I can improve my performance in other areas that are meaningful for my life strategy.
name: Strategy and your life journal entry #4

description:
Please prepare the following for class today:

1) Read the Shein Case on fast fashion

2) Complete Canvas Quiz #6

3) Complete Strategy Journal Entry #4
name: 3/24 Deliverables

description:

2) Complete Case Question Writeup #10

3) Complete Strategy Journal Entry #5
name: 3/31 Deliverables

description:
1) Why has Disney been successful for so long? What is fundamental to their corporate strategy? 

2) What did Michael Eisner do to rejuvenate Disney? Support your analysis using quantitative and qualitative data from the case. 

3) Does the CapCities/ABC merger make strong strategic sense? If you were a consultant for Eisner, what would you recommend and why?

4) Disney has expanded horizontally, vertically, and geographically far beyond the classic Disney of the past. A list of their major acquisitions can be found here https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_acquisitions_by_Disney Links to an external site., and a map of Disney today here: 

https://www.titlemax.com/discovery-center/money-finance/companies-disney-owns-worldwide/

Links to an external site.

Has Disney diversified too far in recent years? Share your thoughts in a few paragraphs. Specifically note which acquisitions make strong strategic sense to you and where you think Disney may have gone too far and why.
name: Case Writeup #10: Disney

description:
What is your “mouse” at the center of your “corporate” strategy? Are you investing in it enough? Are you deploying and exploiting it in all of your different activities to magnify the unique talents and gifts you have been blessed with?

D&C 46:9 (on spiritual gifts)

For verily I say unto you, they are given for the benefit of those who love me and keep all my commandments, and him that seeketh so to do; that all may be benefited that seek or that ask of me....

description: This grade is for the presentation of your final team project in class. There will be a separate grade for the submitted slide deck.
name: Final Group Presentation

description: Please upload your final group presentation (including appendix if any in a single file) here. This is the presentation you are also required to use in class on your presentation day.
name: Final Group Slides

description:
Throughout the course I have invited you to use the lenses and tools of strategy to better understand your own life. Ultimately, "this life is the time for men to prepare to meet God (Alma 34:32)", and consequently you should not reserve your best thinking and efforts for only your career. Prayerfully approach this exercise, asking for revelation about YOU, and then listen with your mind and your heart to the questions and answers that will come. For this assignment, submit to me a one page (or more) reflection of insights and thoughts you have had throughout the semester about how strategy applies to your life. Because strategy is about asking the "right" questions, it is OK if you don't (yet) have the answers to new questions that have arisen while thinking through this exercise.

Please also prepare one short insight to bring to class to share for April 6th.

My hope is that this exercise will help you in your efforts to increase in wisdom, to develop into a more Christlike leader, and in your efforts to pursue eternal life.

D&C 6:7

7 Seek not for riches but for wisdom, and behold, the mysteries of God shall be unfolded unto you, and then shall you be made rich. Behold, he that hath eternal life is rich.
name: Strategy and your life memo

Your company does not need to sign this memo. The memo is due at the start of finals week.

description: Per the syllabus, you are allowed two unexcused absences (for any reason) before attendance begins to negatively affect your grade. After you have exhausted your two free absences, each unapproved absence will lower your attendance participation score by 7.5 points (5% of the total). If you come later or leave early, you will receive a 50% deduction for that day's participation points. There are 20 sessions where attendance will be assessed and 150 points total. So each session is worth 7.5 points. The TA helps me to track attendance and participation for each session.
name: Attendance
